← all tutorials

Eventual Consistency — interactive 📖 EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY.md 📄 eventual_consistency.py

📖 Full guide (.md, green) — the long-form written explanation.  📄 Source code (.py, orange) — the ground-truth logic this page is verified against.
A write is a rumor; replicas spread it by gossip. Watch it propagate round by round until every replica agrees — and watch the stale reads and reorders that happen during convergence. The gossip engine here is the byte-identical twin of eventual_consistency.py (same LCG, same merge), so the green check: OK below confirms a match.

① Gossip propagation — step the rounds, watch the rumor spread

What to do: pick N replicas and an anti-entropy mechanism, write X=1@v1 to node 0, then step the rounds. Each round the infected (updated) replicas gossip. Pick a node to read from — while gossip hasn't reached it, the read glows red (STALE).
round 0 / 0 · infected 1 / 8
fresh (X=0@v0) infected (has X=1@v1) stale read target gossip exchange (delivered) exchange (redundant)

② Convergence time — the epidemic curve (rounds vs infected)

What this shows: push-pull gossip spreads a write like an epidemic — the infected count grows geometrically, so reaching all N nodes takes ~log₂(N) rounds. Drag N (up to 2000): 10× more nodes costs only ~3 extra rounds. The dashed line is the bare log₂(N) reference.
converged in 9 rounds · log₂(N)=9.97
The logistic SHAPE — slow start, explosive middle, slow tail — is the signature of epidemic spreading (Demers 1987). Same curve as a rumor or a virus moving through a population.

③ Reordered updates — a value can go 0 → 2 → 1 (backwards)

What to do: two writes, X=1@v1 to A then X=2@v2 to B (the newer one). Gossip hands replica C the newer write first. Toggle the merge rule: naive lets the value reverse (0→2→1); LWW rejects the older write so C holds at 2.
Without an order-independent merge, the FINAL value depends on chance message ordering → no convergence. LWW (by version) and CRDTs make the outcome independent of delivery order.
check: … JS gossip recompute matches eventual_consistency.py: 3-replica push (seed 1115) → 2 rounds; 1000-node push-pull (seed 42) → 9 rounds; both converge to (1,1).
